sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 6

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 6:1
items.html:
(to the question) Esh Nichpefet [bent fire]
The Gevurot [judgments], when they are in their completeness, are called luminaries of fire [Me'orei HaEsh], in the way that the Hasadim [mercies] are called luminaries of light [Me'orei HaOhr]. Then the Gevurot stand at a level above the Hasadim, in the secret of the verse "a woman of valor is the crown of her husband." And because of the greatness of their level, if the Partzuf is still in the discernment of Katnut [smallness], it must receive them only from below upward — that is, in clothing by way of ascent — so that they do not pass into the spreading of the Masach [screen], from it downward, in the way of clothing in a Guf [body]. For when it receives them from above downward, they harm it. Then those Gevurot are called "bent fire" [Esh Nichpefet]; that is, instead of serving as before from below upward, they were bent and came from above downward. This is the meaning of the bent fire that harmed. (p. 778, Item 68)
